full_response:
Let  

 U a   : a future-directed timelike 4-velocity  
 U a U a = −1                        (normalisation)  

be given in a spacetime with metric g_{ab} (signature −+++).  
Define the projection „onto the space orthogonal to U a“

 P_{ab} = g_{ab} + U_a U_b.                                        (1)

For convenience raise the first index with the metric:

 P^{a}{}_b = g^{ac}P_{cb} = δ^{a}{}_b + U^{a}U_b.                 (2)

A)  The projected vector V⊥ a is orthogonal to U a.  
 Set V⊥ a ≡ P^{a}{}_b V^{b}.  Then

 V⊥ a U_a = (P^{a}{}_b V^{b}) U_a = V^{b}(P^{a}{}_b U_a).         (3)

Evaluate the bracket:

 P^{a}{}_b U_a = (δ^{a}{}_b + U^{a}U_b)U_a
        = U_b + (U^{a}U_a)U_b
        = U_b + (−1)U_b = 0.                                 (4)

Insert (4) into (3):

 V⊥ a U_a = V^{b}·0 = 0.                                         (5)

Therefore V⊥ a is indeed orthogonal to U a.

B)  P^{a}{}_b is idempotent: once a vector is projected, the operator does nothing further.

Compute the product of two projectors:

 P^{a}{}_b P^{b}{}_c
 = (δ^{a}{}_b + U^{a}U_b)(δ^{b}{}_c + U^{b}U_c)
 = δ^{a}{}_c + U^{a}U_c + U^{a}U_c + (U^{a}U_b)(U^{b}U_c)
 = δ^{a}{}_c + 2U^{a}U_c + (U^{b}U_b)U^{a}U_c
 = δ^{a}{}_c + 2U^{a}U_c − U^{a}U_c                (using U^{b}U_b = −1)
 = δ^{a}{}_c + U^{a}U_c
 = P^{a}{}_c.                                                     (6)

Idempotency immediately implies

 P^{a}{}_b V⊥ b = P^{a}{}_b P^{b}{}_c V^{c} = P^{a}{}_c V^{c} = V⊥ a. (7)

Thus once a vector has been projected, applying P again leaves it unchanged.

\boxed{\,V_\perp^{\,a}=P^{a}{}_{b}V^{b}\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
V_\perp^{\,a}U_{a}=0\ \text{and}\ P^{a}{}_{b}V_\perp^{\,b}=V_\perp^{\,a}\,}

Confidence: 0.86
